title: Goroutine Pools, Context Causes, Backpressure, and Shutdown · Execution Trace: Context Cause Tree
summary: A goroutine is launched per URL, workers close shared channels, cancellation stops fetch but not parsing or writes, and main returns before cleanup. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go context cause tree increment in bounded leak-free Go crawl pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pr…
objectives: Derive owner, run, item, attempt, deadline, and shutdown contexts and preserve the first meaningful cancellation cause.
Assign channel creation, senders, receiver, closer, buffering rationale, and cancellation behavior without double close or blocked send.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
